free_text: Experimental studies on excitation functions of the
proton-induced activation reactions on yttrium
Cyclotron and Radioisotope Center
Main reference. See also TITLE and AUTHOR.
M.S.Uddin+, Proc. of the 2004 Symposium on Nuclear
Data
- Chemical-form of target is element.
- Physical-form of target is solid.
- Target-thickness is 49.1 mg/cm**2.
Irradiated with 50 MeV (165 nA for 30 min) and
80 MeV (45nA for 70 min)
Stacks of several identical groups containing
high purity thin yttrium foils
Started 1 day after the end of bombardment
K=110 MeV AVF cyclotron at Cyclotron
and Radioisotope Center
To measure residual activity
Total uncertinty (5-10%)
Maximum uncertainty in monitor flux (3-5%)
Uncertainty in efficiency calibration of
gamma-ray
Uncertainty in foil thickness
Maximum uncertainty of the statistical error

[Y.N. Shubin et al., IAEA-NDS-204 (1998)]

cross-section was negligible.
